title: Chaeshin: Persistent Recursive Case Memory for Tool-Using LLM Agents
description: Chaeshin is a persistent memory layer for tool-using LLM agents based on case-based reasoning. Each case stores a Tool Graph; nodes can themselves unfold into child cases, giving a graph-of-graphs structure with no fixed decomposition depth. Outcomes are tri-state (success / failure / pending), with success and failure transitions reserved for an explicit user verdict. Editing a parent layer's graph cascades to descendants — children whose anchor node was removed are automatically flipped back to pending for review. Ships with an MCP server for Claude Code integration, a Next.js observability monitor, and ReAct demos for cooking, lifestyle coaching, and clinical intake domains.
